title: Projection review and canonical adoption are separate authorization states
summary: A reviewed projection now has a separate fail-closed authorization packet that binds exact projection bytes, canonical queue blob identity, source commit, reviewer identity, decision, and bounded M-series scope. A valid packet records approval or rejection only; it cannot permit automatic adoption or canonical queue mutation. Five deterministic Node tests passed before commit.

status_relations.observed: The validator records a bounded review decision while always returning canonical_queue_modified=false and automatic_adoption_permitted=false.
status_relations.inferred: Separating review authorization from mutation prevents evidence review from silently becoming operational authority.
status_relations.proposed: Add a separately reviewed canonical-change proposal that emits a patch for human application but still cannot write ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
status_relations.superseded: The absence of an explicit projection-review authorization format is superseded by this validator and packet contract.
status_relations.unresolved: No authenticated reviewer-signature mechanism or successful workflow artifact currently binds a real human review to this packet.

falsification_or_review_route: Run node --test operations/continuity/reviewed-adoption-authorization.test.mjs and reject this record if changed projection bytes, widened queue scope, automatic adoption permission, or canonical mutation permission are accepted; additionally reject any claim of human authorization until reviewer identity is independently authenticated.
